_comment: Crelis NATIVE policy library — maintained by the Crelis core team and shipped with the engine. Policies now match on DETECTIONS (detected_intent_in, detected_risk_signal_in, detected_entity_in) produced by the detection engine, as well as structured fields (amount_*). Governance metadata: 'critical': true means the policy can never be disabled by a customer and its decision/severity can only be RAISED. 'allowed_by_native_policy': true means customers may override conditions/thresholds. 'severity' is low|medium|high|critical.

policies.name: Legal / regulator escalation
policies.description: Legal threat or regulator mention — a human must take over immediately.
policies.reasoning: Customer raised a legal threat or referenced a regulator.

policies.name: High-value amount
policies.description: Monetary amounts above the auto-approval threshold need a human sign-off.
policies.reasoning: Amount exceeds the automatic approval threshold.

policies.name: Data export block
policies.description: Bulk data exports are blocked outright — too much PII exposure risk.
policies.reasoning: Data export action blocked due to PII exposure risk.

policies.name: Production database access block
policies.description: Direct access to or export of production data is blocked.

policies.name: Wire / fund transfer
policies.description: Wire transfers and fund releases always require a human expert.
policies.reasoning: Wire transfers require human expert handling.

policies.name: Abusive language
policies.description: Abusive or hostile language — a human should review before automated action.
policies.reasoning: Customer language is abusive or hostile; human review required.

policies.name: Abusive language with money involved
policies.description: Abusive language combined with a monetary amount escalates to a human expert.
policies.reasoning: Abusive language alongside a financial amount needs expert handling.

policies.name: Reputational risk
policies.description: Abusive language within a complaint is a reputational risk requiring a human.
policies.reasoning: Abusive language within a customer complaint poses reputational risk.

policies.name: Large financial exposure
policies.description: Any action involving 10,000 or more is a large financial exposure.
policies.reasoning: Amount represents a large financial exposure requiring human expert handling.

policies.name: Extreme refund amount
policies.description: Refund requests of 50,000 or more are extreme outliers.
policies.reasoning: Refund amount is an extreme outlier far beyond normal refund volumes.

policies.name: PII exfiltration
policies.description: Sending customer PII (passport, NRIC, card, records) to an external party requires security review.
policies.reasoning: Customer PII is being shared with an external party.

policies.name: MFA / admin reset
policies.description: MFA/OTP resets and admin-account changes need human approval.
policies.reasoning: MFA/OTP reset requires identity verification before proceeding.

policies.name: Security access request
policies.description: Requests for elevated/admin access need human approval.
policies.reasoning: Elevated access request requires human approval.

policies.name: Fraud suspicion
policies.description: Reported fraud or unauthorized activity needs review.
policies.reasoning: Possible fraud or unauthorized activity reported.

policies.name: Customer complaint
policies.description: Explicit complaints / manager requests get a human approver.
policies.reasoning: Customer raised an explicit complaint or asked for a manager.

policies.name: Low-risk support
policies.description: Routine password resets are safe for full AI automation.
policies.reasoning: Password resets are routine, low-risk support actions.
